tags.qa_notes: Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted to domain names. Wall dive at Pitons confirmed. Depth 50m is maximum reported.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. Shore reef confirmed at Anse Chastanet. Shallow starting 15ft.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. Pinnacles at Anse Cochon confirmed by SMMA and Scuba St. Lucia.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. PADI blog describes as 'probably most visually stunning dive site' with four volcanic peaks rising from depths to near surface.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted to domain names.

Re-audited 2026-05-24. Lesleen M confirmed: 165ft (~50m) freighter, scuttled October 1986, Anse Cochon bay, dept of fisheries. Rests on even keel. Deck at 9m, max depth 18m (60ft). Cargo hold accessible. Rudder and propeller intact. Marine life: turtles, lobsters, moray eels, spider crabs, shrimp, schools of red snapper, angelfish, barracuda confirmed.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. Shore reef at Jalousie Bay (Sugar Beach) between Pitons confirmed.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. Anse La Raye wall confirmed: starts at 18m/60ft, descends to 34m/110ft per SMMA/DIVEIN. Ti Kaye Resort operates dives here.

Re-audited 2026-05-24. Daini Koyomaru confirmed: Japanese dredger, scuttled September 1996 by St Lucia government as artificial reef. Length: sources vary between 74m (DeeperBlue) and 75m (divingaway.com). Originally upright at 32m, knocked onto starboard side by Hurricane Lenny 1999. Current depth: various sources say max 20m (resting on side) to 33m. Description notes both figures. Marine life: pufferfish, French angelfish, barracuda, moray eels, turtles confirmed.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. Shore reef at Soufrière area confirmed.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. Drift dive at Pitons area confirmed. Advanced due to current.
Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source reformatted. Wall dive at Grand Caille confirmed by DiveWorld and SMMA.

tags.name: El Pirata Shipwreck
tags.qa_notes: Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source 'perplexity' removed - perplexity AI is NOT a valid source. No independent dive-specific sources found for 'El Pirata Shipwreck' on St Lucia's southern coast. OSM data confirms wreck exists (visible at high and low tide). Depth_max 20m from OSM. Cannot validate factual claims without real sources. Marked unvalidated.

tags.name: Fairyland
tags.qa_notes: Re-audited 2026-05-24. Fairyland confirmed as Divemaster favorite with strong currents keeping corals vibrant. One of the most colorful reefs in St Lucia confirmed by DIVEIN and Scuba St Lucia.

tags.name: Maria Island Reef
tags.qa_notes: Re-audited 2026-05-24. validation_source 'perplexity' removed - perplexity AI is NOT a valid source. Maria Islands (also spelled 'Marie Islands') are a confirmed nature reserve off St Lucia's south coast. Reef exists (OSM natural=reef confirmed). No dive-specific sources found for the dive site specifically. Marked unvalidated until real sources found.
